title: Frameworks hide prompts — understand what's underneath

body.problem: You adopt an agent framework to move fast, but when something goes wrong, you can't debug it. The framework's abstractions hide the actual prompts and API responses. You also end up adding complexity the framework makes easy but your use case doesn't need.
body.solution: Start by using LLM APIs directly — most patterns need only a few lines of code. If you do use a framework (Claude Agent SDK, Strands, Rivet, Vellum), always understand the underlying code. Incorrect assumptions about what's under the hood are a top source of errors Anthropic sees in customer implementations.
body.keyTakeaway: Frameworks are fine as accelerators, but never as black boxes. If you can't explain what prompt is being sent and what response is expected at each step, you'll struggle to debug, optimize, or simplify.
